---
title: "CpG_peaks"
author: "G Sejourne"
date: "2026-01-26"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
install.packages("xlsx")
library(xlsx)
library(GenomicRanges)

```

## R Markdown

This is an R Markdown document. Markdown is a simple formatting syntax for authoring HTML, PDF, and MS Word documents. For more details on using R Markdown see <http://rmarkdown.rstudio.com>.

When you click the **Knit** button a document will be generated that includes both content as well as the output of any embedded R code chunks within the document. You can embed an R code chunk like this:

```{r cars}
union_peaks <- read.table("union_promoters_TSS.bed")
ucsc_cpg <- read.table("UCSC_CpG.bed")

# GRanges object 1 (CpG)
gr1 <- GRanges(
  seqnames = ucsc_cpg$V1,
  ranges = IRanges(start = ucsc_cpg$V2, end = ucsc_cpg$V3),
  score = ucsc_cpg$V4
)

# GRanges object 2 (union peaks)
gr2 <- GRanges(
  seqnames = union_peaks$V1,
  ranges = IRanges(start = union_peaks$V2, end = union_peaks$V3),
  strand = union_peaks$V6,
  gene_name = union_peaks$V4
)

# Find overlaps between gr1 (query) and gr2 (subject)
gr2_subset <- subsetByOverlaps(gr2, gr1)
print(gr2_subset)

union_cpg <- as.data.frame(gr2_subset)
head(union_cpg)
```


```{r cars}
bed <- data.frame(
    chrom  = union_cpg$seqnames,
    start  = union_cpg$start,
    end    = union_cpg$end,
    name   = union_cpg$gene_name,
    strand = union_cpg$strand)

## ---- write BED ----
write.table(
  bed,
  file = "ucsc_union_cpg.bed",
  sep = "\t",
  quote = FALSE,
  row.names = FALSE,
  col.names = FALSE
)


```

## Including Plots
